drafts.draft: The boring middle layer of AI for small business (follow-up, scheduling, review chasing) is where the actual money is. The rest is theatre.
Every plumber I've talked to has the same bottleneck. Not leads, not pricing. A phone they can't pick up while they're under a sink.
A receptionist that costs 30k a year and one that costs 30 a month don't compete. The cheap one just shows up at 2am too.

drafts.target_text: In defense of the unnamed bank, His Holiness is under U.S. law a politically exposed person as head of state, so a wide range of routine things need enhanced due diligence.
drafts.draft: Genuine question for the compliance side: do PEP rules actually require a freeze, or is the bank using PEP as cover for a UI flag they don't know how to clear?
What's the bottleneck this actually unblocks? Longer context windows, faster tokens, or just more concurrent agents per account?

drafts.target_text: I'm at the Claude w/ Code event in San Francisco, and I'll be live blogging the keynote here.
drafts.draft: Watching the live blog. What's the one announcement you think the room underreacted to? Those are usually the ones that actually ship.

drafts.target_text: I'll be at Code with Claude all day. Giving a talk on the main stage at 530pm PT, tune in on the livestream.
drafts.draft: What's the talk about? Trying to decide whether to set an alarm in London for it.

drafts.target_text: I talk to a lot of people who consider themselves very risk averse. They keep cash, rush to pay off low interest debt, avoid investing. Yet they are exposed.
drafts.draft: The exposure people don't see: zero income diversification. Salary stops, all the cash hoarding in the world buys you maybe 18 months. That's the real risk.

drafts.target_text: Money advice nobody told you: the only way to make a lot of money is to create a lot of value. No one is going to pay you just because they like you.
drafts.draft: The corollary nobody says either: most people undercharge for the value they already create. Pricing is the cheapest way to make more money.

drafts.target_text: New fun thing I did to secure my VPS: installed Cloudflare Tunnel. I already had 443 inbound firewall limited to Cloudflare IP range, but this is even tighter.
drafts.draft: Tunnel kills the public IP attack surface entirely, which is the real win. Out of curiosity, did latency change noticeably for your highest-traffic regions?

drafts.target_text: Discord's 'are you human' captcha went from 'a human can solve it immediately' to 'sit down and do some work for it.' Captchas were meant to be dead simple for humans, hard for bots.
drafts.draft: The captcha arms race ended when bots got better than humans at the puzzles. Now they're just a tax on real users to slow signups slightly. Time for something else.

drafts.target_text: We're bringing in real-time licensed financial data into our API. This allows developers to perform tool calls that pull real-time data along with verified web sources for financial agents.
drafts.draft: Which exchanges and which latency bands? Real-time means very different things to a finance agent depending on whether you mean tick data or 15-min delayed.

drafts.target_text: The entire AI-native opportunity map in a 2x2 matrix. Most people build in the wrong quadrant. Top left is too simple, already commoditized. Bottom left isn't painful enough.
drafts.draft: Curious which quadrant you'd put 'small business operational AI' in. Painful, not commoditized, but distribution-hard. The matrix usually misses distribution as the third axis.

drafts.target_text: I've started using a new term: Tragedy Apps. Apps you loved 18 months ago. Still functional. Still shipping. But haven't kept up in the Age of AI.
drafts.draft: Tragedy Apps usually have one thing in common: a founder who shipped fast pre-AI and now treats AI as a feature instead of a re-platform. The category, not the model, gets re-rated.

drafts.target_text: What creates the most leverage in a business today? Automation, audience, brand, or distribution?
drafts.draft: Distribution. The other three multiply zero if nobody knows you exist. Distribution is the only one that compounds while you sleep.
